title: Seven Surprises by the Shore
synopsis: When a magical living teapot named Kettle discovers a new neighbor with mysterious habits on the pebble beach, seven small gifts arriving throughout the week reveal wonderful secrets about making, building, and friendship as a summer storm approaches.

pages.text:
PLIP-PLOP! PLIP-PLOP! The first raindrops of the summer storm splashed onto the smooth pebbles. Kettle, a magical living teapot with sky-blue porcelain, painted yellow flowers, a curling handle arm, and steam curling from the spout like hair, loved the sound the drops made on the beach stones.

'Oh my!' she bubbled cheerfully, her steam swirling faster. 'What a wonderful day for a waddle along the shore!'

But wait - what was that? Can you see it too, dear reader? Something small and shiny sat between two round gray pebbles.

illustration.description: A magical living teapot with sky-blue porcelain, painted yellow flowers, a curling handle arm, and steam curling from the spout like hair discovers a small shiny object among gray pebbles on a beach. Rain drops are visible falling from dark clouds above. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Kettle finding the first mysterious gift on the rainy pebble beach

pages.text:
'A tiny silver spring!' Kettle exclaimed, gently picking it up with her handle arm. 'Boing-boing-boing!' The spring bounced merrily in her grasp.

But where had it come from? The little teapot looked around the empty beach. The storm clouds rumbled overhead - GRUMBLE-RUMBLE! - but she saw no one else.

'How curious!' she said, tucking the spring safely beside her painted yellow flowers. 'Someone must have dropped this. But who?'

illustration.description: A magical living teapot with sky-blue porcelain, painted yellow flowers, a curling handle arm, and steam curling from the spout like hair holds a tiny silver spring that's bouncing. Dark storm clouds fill the sky over the pebble beach. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Kettle examining the bouncing silver spring under stormy clouds

pages.text:
PITTER-PATTER! PITTER-PATTER! Tuesday brought more rain, and - oh! - another surprise. This time, a small brass gear lay gleaming between the wet pebbles.

'Tick-tick-tick!' it seemed to whisper as Kettle picked it up.

Wednesday: WHOOSH! A tiny wooden wheel rolled right up to her feet!

Thursday: CLINK! A miniature copper pipe.

Friday: WHIRR! A set of colorful wires, all twisted together like a rainbow braid.

illustration.description: A magical living teapot with sky-blue porcelain, painted yellow flowers, a curling handle arm, and steam curling from the spout like hair surrounded by various small mechanical objects - gears, wheels, pipes, and colorful wires - scattered on wet pebbles. Storm clouds continue to gather. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Kettle collecting mysterious mechanical gifts throughout the week

pages.text:
'Goodness gracious!' Kettle bubbled each day, her steam dancing with excitement. 'More treasures! But who could be leaving them?'

Saturday brought the biggest surprise yet - THUMP! A small wooden box with tiny hinges.

'What strange gifts,' the kind teapot wondered aloud. 'Springs and gears, wheels and wires, pipes and boxes. What could they all be for?'

The storm was growing stronger now. CRASH-BOOM! Thunder rolled across the darkening sky.

illustration.description: A magical living teapot with sky-blue porcelain, painted yellow flowers, a curling handle arm, and steam curling from the spout like hair looking thoughtfully at a small wooden box while lightning flashes in the dramatic storm clouds above the pebble beach. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Kettle pondering the wooden box as the storm intensifies

pages.text:
WHOOOOSH! Sunday's storm wind howled across the beach like a wild thing. Kettle clutched all her mysterious gifts tightly - the spring, the gear, the wheel, the pipe, the wires, and the wooden box.

'Oh my!' she gasped as the wind tried to knock her over. 'This storm is getting quite fierce!'

Then - SURPRISE! - from behind a large driftwood log popped the tiniest mechanical mouse you ever did see! Click-whirr-buzz!

illustration.description: A magical living teapot with sky-blue porcelain, painted yellow flowers, a curling handle arm, and steam curling from the spout like hair meets a tiny mechanical mouse emerging from behind driftwood on the windy beach. The storm is at its peak with dramatic clouds and wind. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Kettle meeting the mechanical mouse during the fierce storm

pages.text:
'Squeak-squeak!' chirped the little mouse. 'Oh no! Oh dear! The storm scattered all my invention pieces across the beach! I've been looking everywhere!'

'YOUR invention pieces?' Kettle's steam curled with delight. 'You mean these wonderful treasures I've been finding?'

'Yes, yes!' The mouse's copper whiskers twitched. 'I'm building a weather-watching machine! Spring for measuring, gears for turning, wheels for rolling, pipes for collecting raindrops, wires for connecting, and a box to hold it all!'

illustration.description: A magical living teapot with sky-blue porcelain, painted yellow flowers, a curling handle arm, and steam curling from the spout like hair happily shows the collected mechanical parts to an excited tiny mechanical mouse on the pebble beach. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Kettle and the mouse examining all the invention pieces together

pages.text:
'What a marvelous invention!' Kettle bubbled joyfully, handing over each precious piece. 'I kept them safe for you all week long!'

'Thank you, thank you!' squeaked the grateful mouse, already beginning to snap and click and whirr the pieces together. 'Would you like to help me finish it?'

'Oh, yes indeed!' Steam danced from Kettle's spout as they worked together, building and creating while the storm raged around them.

Click-snap-whirr-buzz! Soon the weather-watcher was complete, measuring and turning and collecting - just in time to catch every drop of the summer storm!

'Making things together,' Kettle said with a happy sigh, 'is the very best kind of magic.'

And don't you think so too, dear reader?

illustration.description: A magical living teapot with sky-blue porcelain, painted yellow flowers, a curling handle arm, and steam curling from the spout like hair and a tiny mechanical mouse work together assembling a whimsical weather-watching contraption made from all the collected parts, while the summer storm swirls dramatically around them on the pebble beach. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
illustration.alt: Kettle and the mouse completing their weather-watching invention together in the storm

morals: Keeping things safe for others shows kindness

coverPrompt.description: A magical living teapot with sky-blue porcelain, painted yellow flowers, a curling handle arm, and steam curling from the spout like hair stands on a pebble beach with dark storm clouds gathering overhead. Small colorful gifts are scattered on the smooth stones around her. Gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes.
coverPrompt.alt: Kettle the magical teapot on a stormy beach with mysterious gifts

questions.question: What was the first gift Kettle found on Monday?
questions.explanation: The first gift Kettle discovered was a tiny silver spring that went 'boing-boing-boing!'

questions.question: Who had been leaving the gifts on the beach?
questions.explanation: The tiny mechanical mouse had lost all the pieces for its weather-watching invention during the storm.

questions.question: What was the mouse trying to build?
questions.explanation: The mouse was building a weather-watching machine to measure and collect information about storms.

questions.question: How many gifts did Kettle collect during the week?
questions.explanation: Kettle found six gifts: a spring, gear, wheel, pipe, colorful wires, and a wooden box.

questions.question: What did Kettle learn about making things?
choices: Making things together is the best magic
questions.explanation: Kettle discovered that 'making things together is the very best kind of magic' when she helped the mouse.
